name: Phil Spitalny
abstract: Phil Spitalny (November 7, 1890 – October 11, 1970) was a Russian-born American musician, music critic, composer, and bandleader heard often on radio during the 1930s and 1940s. He rose to fame after he led an all-female orchestra, a novelty at the time.

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: Phil Spitalny's got a band at the Edgewater Beach and he needs a jazz trumpet player... so, I went there and I stayed there a very short time

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Another band was Clyde Trask. Clyde Trask was a bandleader who was an arranger who had arranged for Paul Whiteman. No, not Paul Whiteman. Phil Spitalny.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Grosz
about_quotes.evidence: I went to see bands. I listened to everything. Phil Spitalny and his All-Girl Orchestra... I’d go to see anything just to see how they did it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Grosz interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: And let's see, well, the inevitable Phil Spitalny and of course Freddie Martin, and Vincent Lopez.

about_quotes.speaker: "Wild" Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: I got him in the band at the Edgewater Beach with Phil Spitalny years later
